title: armeria vulgaris var. pubescens
intro: Armeria vulgaris var. pubescens, also known as the pubescent thrift or hairy thrift, is a variety of flowering plant in the primrose family (Primulaceae), characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Armeria vulgaris var. pubescens include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against bacterial and fungal infections. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to promote skin health and wound recovery.
